issues.message: The pressed-state section invents behavior the React component does not have. Upstream pressed styles only apply when the root itself is rendered as a button (`.seed-page-banner__root:is(button)`), but `PageBanner.Root` renders a dismissible `div`; pressing `Button`/`CloseButton` does not darken the root background.

The prefix-icon guidance is wrong for the React API. PageBanner styling targets the shared `seed-prefix-icon` slot class, so telling consumers to put a raw first-child `<Icon>`/SVG here will miss the prefix slot sizing and margins; this needs to point to the shared `PrefixIcon` wrapper instead.

The base-token table is not copied from upstream: rootage and generated CSS both define `asset.maxWidth` as the literal `160px`, not `$dimension.x40`. This teaches the skill a token that does not exist in the source spec.

This says `ContentPlaceholder.Asset` without `Root` fails preset lookup, but the React implementation falls back to `contentPlaceholderAssetPresetMap[parentProps?.type ?? "default"]`. Standalone `Asset` renders the default preset instead of failing.

The definition line fabricates variant names `neutral`, `positive`, and `informative`; upstream only defines `neutralWeak`, `positiveWeak`, `informativeWeak`, `warningWeak`, `warningSolid`, `criticalWeak`, and `criticalSolid`.
This markdown link is broken: `../decision-matrices/feedback-channel.md` does not exist in the references tree, so AI consumers cannot follow the promised decision matrix.

The index summary reintroduces a nonexistent `indeterminate` API surface. Upstream `ProgressCircleRootProps` explicitly omits the `indeterminate` variant prop; React consumers switch modes with `value`/`minValue`/`maxValue` instead.

The defaults section is incorrect: the generated text recipe defaults `textStyle` to `t5Regular`, so omitting `textStyle` does not leave `Text` on inherited typography. This documents the wrong default behavior for AI consumers.
